programmes.show_description: Escape in Time: The Avengers attempt to track down criminals who have mysteriously vanished. Is the case linked to the deaths of secret agents and a Tudor mansion?
Your Hit Parade: A new shipment of records has come in and Col Potter has promised Radar that he can play the records over the PA system to lift everyone's spirits.
What's Up, Doc?: Margaret, believing she is pregnant, undergoes a rabbit test administered by Hawkeye. A patient who desperately wants to go home holds Charles and BJ at gunpoint.
Mail Call Three: Five sacks of mail arrive at the 4077th, three weeks late. Radar receives a letter from his mother informing him of her new boyfriend.
Rumpole and the Blind Tasting: Rumpole's latest case sees him defending Hugh Timson who is suspected of harbouring stolen wine. Meanwhile, Rumpole has concerns over his new pupil.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Keep Smiling: In one of his most emotional assignments yet, Jonathan is sent to help his widow regain her love for life. Because of his new appearance and name, she does not recognise him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Last Assignment: Jonathan and Mark's latest assignment involves a wayward angel who has been on probation for two centuries and can't help breaking the rules while spreading happiness.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Bully Boys: Following Reverend Alden's lead, the Walnut Grove townsfolk try to turn the other cheek when the bad behaviour of the Galender brothers causes tempers to flare.
Blizzard: Miss Beadle dismisses her students early on Christmas Eve, unaware that the children will soon be caught in a howling blizzard.
I'll Ride the Wind: Mr Edwards' son must make a tough decision: realise his dream of being a writer or marry his one true love, Mary Ingalls.
Quarantine: A deadly mountain fever hits the neighbouring town of Elmsville, and Mr Edwards unknowingly carries it back to Walnut Grove.
Little Women: Mrs Oleson rewrites a play so Nellie can have the starring role, but shy Ginny Clark steals the show.
Injun Kid: Joseph Stokes is rejected by his grandpa because his daughter married a Native American. However, when Joseph faces some school bullies, his grandpa's opinion changes.
To Live with Fear: Part 1: Mary is seriously injured and in need of an expensive operation when she is accidentally kicked by a startled horse. Part 1 of 2.
To Live with Fear: Part 2: Charles accepts a dangerous job in order to pay for Mary's ongoing medical treatment. Part 2 of 2.
The Wisdom of Solomon: Yearning to escape the hard life that sent his father to an early grave, eleven-year-old Solomon Henry runs away from his family and seeks solace with the Ingalls.
The Music Box: Nellie discovers that Laura has 'borrowed' a music box from her room and decides to blackmail her.
The Election: As a prank, the boys of Walnut Grove school nominate the object of their practical jokes, Elmer Dobkins, to run for class president against Mary Ingalls and Nellie Oleson.
Comedy classic with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy. Our bickering duo head to London to celebrate their anniversary, but there's more chaos than canoodling when George is mistaken for a hitman. (1980)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Comedy classic with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy. Our bickering duo head to London to celebrate their anniversary, but there's more chaos than canoodling when George is mistaken for a hitman. (1980)
You Can't Be Too Careful: Thriller with James Callis. Guy falls in love all over again when he runs into an ex-girlfriend. But the feeling isn't mutual, his passion descends into obsession.
The Fallen Curtain: Wartime drama starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Fiona Dolman and Sylvia Syms. Forbidden love leads to devastating tragedy when a teenager is sent to her aunt's rural home.
Dark thriller starring Denzel Washington and Angelina Jolie. Can a paralysed forensics expert and a rookie cop unravel a serial killer's cryptic crime-scene clues before he strikes again? (1999)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Dark thriller starring Denzel Washington and Angelina Jolie. Can a paralysed forensics expert and a rookie cop unravel a serial killer's cryptic crime-scene clues before he strikes again? (1999)
Getting on?!: After Henry's recent visit to the doctor's, Matthew decides to do more for older people, and invites a local pensioner to come and stay with them.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
To Bind the Wounds: A persistent father attempts to have a memorial made to commemorate his son, a soldier killed in Vietnam, but he is met with indifference from the townspeople.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Heaven on Earth: Convinced he's responsible for two tragedies, Mark demands that Jonathan erase everything that's happened to him recently and allow him to create his own heaven on Earth.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Missing: Phil and Helen await the birth of their baby, while Laura and her parents await the arrival of Mike. Will Laura's parents first impressions of Mike last?
The Celebration: After years of hard work, the Walton family are finally out of debt. Meanwhile, Corabeth tries to play matchmaker for the new preacher.
The Rumor: Elizabeth's vivid imagination threatens to get her in trouble when she starts a rumour about another family.
Spring Fever: Mamie Baldwin thinks her declining rosebush is a sign of her own impending death. Meanwhile, Jim-Bob and Ben fight over each other's girlfriends.
The Festival: Jason encounters racism when he and Josh audition for the Spring Festival, and Elizabeth faces a battle to win a boy's affection.
The Anniversary: John and Olivia celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary, and Mary Ellen and Curt both fight temptation during their enforced separation.
The Family Tree: With Jason's help, Verdie searches for her family history. Meanwhile, Elizabeth exaggerates the truth when writing to her soldier pen pal.
The Ordeal: Part 1: Elizabeth falls while standing on top of a wood pile and fractures her leg. Part 1 of 2.
The Ordeal: Part 2: Elizabeth falls while standing on top of a wood pile and fractures her leg. Part 2 of 2.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
...Robbery: Classroom chaos with Frankie Howerd. A gang of crooks have stashed their loot in an empty country house. But their thievery is derailed when the girls of St Trinian's move in... (1966)
Action comedy starring Arnold Schwarzenegger. To trap an elusive criminal, a hard-edged LA cop goes undercover as a kindergarten teacher. But is he tough enough to survive Astoria Elementary? (1990)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Cult comedy starring Joe Pesci and Marisa Tomei. New Yorkers Bill and Stan are in deep trouble in an Alabama jail. But will justice also go south when Brooklyn's newest legal eagle arrives? (1992)
